title: 7 Myths About Rental Agents That Keep New Agents Broke
dek: The rental side of real estate is buried under myths, and every one of them costs new agents money. Here are the seven I hear most, and what's actually true.
heroAlt: 7 Myths About Rental Agents That Keep New Agents Broke

blocks.text: Myth 1: Rentals are just practice for sales

blocks.html: Rentals are a business, not a batting cage. Practiced seriously, with systems and relationships, leasing supports six-figure businesses in major markets. Calling it practice is how the industry excuses never teaching it.

blocks.text: Myth 2: The commissions are too small to matter

blocks.html: One luxury lease pays roughly one month's rent, which downtown in a city like Chicago can be a several-thousand-dollar event, and rental deals close in days rather than months. Small checks that stack quickly beat big checks that arrive rarely, especially in year one.

blocks.text: A closed lease beats an open escrow.

blocks.html: Renters decide in days, sign binding leases, refer their relocating coworkers, and come back when they upgrade or buy. There is no more decisive client in real estate than someone whose lease ends in three weeks.

blocks.text: Myth 4: There's no repeat business in rentals

blocks.html: The opposite. Renters move every year or two. Serve one well and you've booked their next move, their coworker's move, and eventually their home purchase. Rental clients compound faster than any other client type in the industry.

blocks.text: Myth 5: You need to buy leads to get started

blocks.html: The buildings already have the leads. Property managers get asked for agent recommendations constantly. A focused list of building relationships, worked consistently, produces more business than any portal subscription, at a cost of zero dollars.

blocks.text: Myth 6: Luxury clients only work with luxury sales agents

blocks.html: Luxury renters work with whoever is the most prepared professional in the room. Show up early, know the building cold, run a flawless process, and you are the luxury experience, regardless of what your business card says.

blocks.text: Myth 7: You'll get stuck in rentals

blocks.html: Nobody gets stuck with income, reps, and a client list. Rental agents who later add sales arrive with hundreds of negotiations behind them and a book of clients who already trust them. Rentals aren't a trap. They're the funded, experienced route to whatever you want next.
All seven myths get replaced with systems in my free ebook, Luxury Leases, Real Profits: 9 Secrets to Earning Big Without Selling a Home. Better agents win.
